data_out.description: Validation report documenting DTCCSN2 library structure, lipid class coverage, CCS value statistics, and conformance to specifications

domain_knowledge: The DTCCSN2 library contains collision cross section (CCS) values measured under nitrogen gas (N2) for uniformly labeled U13C lipids, serving as an internal calibration standard.

CCS values in ion mobility spectrometry are sensitive to lipid structure, charge state, and measurement conditions; libraries must specify the pressure and gas type under which measurements were conducted.

Lipid class coverage refers to the diversity of structural classes (e.g., PC, PE, PS, TG) represented in the reference library; effective CCS bias correction requires at least a few representatives per class.
The U13C uniform labeling allows internal standardization by coelution with native lipids, eliminating the need for external calibration measurements beyond vendor-specific system tuning.

direct_checks: Verify file 'DTCCSN2_library.csv' (or equivalent lipid library file with .csv, .xlsx, .rda, or .txt extension) exists in github:FelinaHildebrand__MobiLipid repository root or designated data directory
Verify file format is consistent with tabular structure (row_count_equals at least 1 header row plus data rows; field_present for columns: lipid_identifier, CCS_value, lipid_class, isotope_label)
Verify field 'isotope_label' contains substring 'U13C' or '13C' for all library entries (robust to case variation)

Value of field 'CCS_value' is in_range and numeric (non-null, parseable as float); no canonical answer for absolute range without instrument metadata — expert review required for physical plausibility

Verify lipid_class field contains_substring at least one of: 'PC', 'PE', 'PS', 'PG', 'PA', 'PI', 'LPC', 'LPE', 'SM', 'Cer', 'DAG', 'TAG', 'CE' (or equivalent LIPID MAPS nomenclature) — multiple defensible class schemes exist
Verify row_count_equals is greater than 10 (minimum viable library size for CCS bias assessment across multiple lipid classes)
expert_review: Review CCS values for physical plausibility: confirm they fall within expected range for singly or doubly charged lipid ions under nitrogen drift gas (typical range ~200–600 Ų for phospholipids, ~300–800 Ų for triacylglycerols); requires domain knowledge of ion mobility physics

Review lipid class coverage: confirm library includes sufficient diversity of lipid classes (phospholipids, glycerolipids, sphingolipids, etc.) to support the stated use case of 'internal standardization across multiple lipid classes'

Cross-check library entries against abstract claim that DTCCSN2 library is 'newly established': verify entries are not simple duplicates of existing public CCS databases (e.g., LipidBlast, Lipidomics Workbench); inspect metadata or publication date if available
Validate row consistency: confirm all rows have non-null CCS_value, lipid_class, and isotope_label fields; flag any missing or malformed entries

finding: MobiLipid provides a newly established DTCCSN2 library for U13C labeled lipids distributed together with the code.
methodology_summary: Clone or download the MobiLipid repository from GitHub.
Locate and open the DTCCSN2 library file and inspect its file format (e.g., CSV, TSV, or R data object).
Parse and extract all records, verifying presence of required fields (lipid identifier, class, CCS value, measurement conditions).
Enumerate and count lipid classes and verify CCS value distributions are consistent with expected ranges for ion mobility data.
Compare observed library composition against specifications stated in project documentation and paper abstract.
Validation: confirm file loads without error, all required fields are populated, CCS values are numeric and physically plausible, and lipid class coverage matches documented scope.

workflow_description: 1. Clone or access the FelinaHildebrand/MobiLipid GitHub repository. 2. Locate and load the DTCCSN2 library file (U13C labeled lipids CCS reference data). 3. Parse the library file format and extract metadata including lipid classes, CCS values, and measurement conditions. 4. Verify that all expected lipid classes are present and that CCS values are numeric and within physically plausible ranges for ion mobility data. 5. Cross-check documented lipid class counts and CCS value distributions against repository documentation and abstract claims. 6. Generate a validation report summarizing library structure, coverage, and any deviations from expected specifications.
